response.justification: The candidate fails to meet the most critical hard requirements for this role. The job mandates 8 years of C++ programming experience, yet the resume shows only approximately 6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments (2014–2015) and an incidental bachelor thesis mention — the primary stack throughout the career is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. There is no evidence of cloud compute platform ownership, large-scale infrastructure architecture leadership, or a formal technical lead role in a cloud or DevOps context. The distributed systems exposure at Dunnhumby is notable but operates at the application layer, not the infrastructure layer the role demands. The candidate is a strong application-layer engineer with good engineering practices, but the qualification gaps are too fundamental for this position.
strengths: Distributed systems exposure at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests/sec with low-latency async I/O patterns
Strong, demonstrable TDD and engineering best practices culture backed by workshops, open-source tooling, and mentoring activity
Broad polyglot background across many environments showing genuine technical adaptability
concerns: C++ experience is critically insufficient — roughly 6 months versus the required 8 years, and it is not the candidate's primary language by any measure
No cloud compute platform or large-scale infrastructure architecture experience; AWS exposure is limited to CDK for application delivery at Optilyz
No formal technical leadership role focused on platform or infrastructure engineering; career trajectory is product/application engineering, not platform engineering
